tickets.title: Add comprehensive API endpoint test coverage
tickets.description:
Create integration tests for all API endpoints across routers (boards, tickets, goals, jobs, revisions, etc.). Currently only 6 test files exist covering limited functionality. Tests should cover:
- CRUD operations for all resources
- State transitions and validation
- Error cases and edge cases
- Board-scoped authorization checks
- Bulk operations (accept, priority updates)

Acceptance criteria:
- Test coverage >80% for all routers
- All endpoints have at least happy path and error case tests
- Tests use pytest fixtures from conftest.py
- Tests verify database state changes correctly
tickets.priority_rationale: High priority - tests are essential for maintaining code quality and preventing regressions as the codebase grows. Current test coverage is minimal.
tickets.notes: Focus on testing routers/ directory first. Use async test patterns with AsyncSession fixtures. Consider using httpx.AsyncClient for API endpoint testing.

tickets.title: Add comprehensive API documentation and README
tickets.description:
Create detailed documentation for the Smart Kanban backend API including:
- README.md with setup instructions, architecture overview, and development guide
- API endpoint documentation (OpenAPI/Swagger is auto-generated but needs enhancement)
- Database schema documentation
- Environment variable reference
- Deployment guide
- Contributing guidelines

Acceptance criteria:
- README.md exists with clear setup instructions
- All environment variables documented in ENV_SETUP.md or README
- API endpoint examples provided
- Architecture diagram or description included
- Database migration guide documented
tickets.priority_rationale: Medium priority - documentation improves developer onboarding and maintainability, but doesn't block functionality.
tickets.notes: Reference existing ENV_SETUP.md. Include examples from CURL_EXAMPLES.md. Document the board-scoped architecture and permission model.

tickets.title: Add database migration validation and rollback tests
tickets.description:
Implement validation for Alembic migrations to prevent data loss and ensure migrations are reversible:
- Add migration tests that verify up/down migrations work correctly
- Validate that migrations don't drop columns without data migration
- Test migration rollback scenarios
- Add pre-migration checks for critical schema changes
- Document migration best practices

Acceptance criteria:
- Test suite validates all migrations can be applied and rolled back
- Migrations include data migration steps where needed
- Pre-migration validation catches dangerous operations
- Migration guide documents rollback procedures
tickets.priority_rationale: High priority - database migrations can cause data loss if not properly tested. Current 12 migration files need validation.
verification: alembic upgrade head && alembic downgrade -1 && alembic upgrade head
tickets.notes: Create tests/test_migrations.py. Use Alembic's testing utilities. Focus on migrations that modify or drop columns (especially in ticket, job, goal tables).

tickets.title: Implement structured logging and request tracing
tickets.description:
Enhance logging throughout the application with structured logging and request tracing:
- Replace print statements and basic logger calls with structured logging
- Add request ID tracking for tracing requests through the system
- Implement correlation IDs for Celery tasks
- Add performance logging for slow operations
- Create log aggregation configuration (JSON format for production)

Acceptance criteria:
- All services use structured logging with context
- Request IDs propagate through FastAPI → services → Celery tasks
- Slow operations (>1s) are logged with timing
- Logs include board_id, ticket_id, goal_id for traceability
- Production logging uses JSON format
tickets.priority_rationale: Medium priority - improves debugging and observability but doesn't block core functionality. Current logging is basic.
tickets.notes: Use structlog or python-json-logger. Add middleware for request ID generation. Update worker.py to include correlation IDs in Celery tasks.

tickets.title: Add health check endpoints and monitoring metrics
tickets.description:
Enhance the existing /health endpoint and add monitoring capabilities:
- Expand /health to check database connectivity, Redis availability, Celery worker status
- Add /metrics endpoint for Prometheus-style metrics
- Track key metrics: job queue depth, ticket state distribution, error rates
- Add readiness and liveness probes for Kubernetes deployment
- Implement health check for board repo_root accessibility

Acceptance criteria:
- /health endpoint returns detailed status of all dependencies
- /metrics endpoint exposes Prometheus-compatible metrics
- Health checks fail fast if critical dependencies unavailable
- Metrics track: active jobs, tickets by state, API request rates, error rates
tickets.priority_rationale: Medium priority - monitoring improves production reliability and debugging, but basic health check already exists.
tickets.notes: Use prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator or similar. Check database, Redis, and Celery broker connectivity. Validate board repo_root paths are accessible.
